uniqueFacts: Stephens County was created at Oklahoma statehood in 1907 from parts of the Chickasaw Nation (Indian Territory) and Comanche County (Oklahoma Territory)
Erle P. Halliburton founded the Halliburton Oil Well Cementing Company in Duncan in 1919; the company grew into one of the world's largest oilfield service corporations
The Chisholm Trail passed directly through Stephens County, and the Old Duncan Store, established circa 1867, was situated along this famous cattle drive route
Two-thirds of Stephens County was originally part of Pickens County in the Chickasaw Nation, while the western third was part of Comanche County in Oklahoma Territory
Stephens County is known as the 'Buckle in the Oil Belt' due to its significant oil and gas production history dating back to 1918
processServingNotes: Stephens County Courthouse is located at 101 S 11th Street in Duncan with Court Clerk office in Suite 301; normal hours are 8:30am to 4:30pm Monday through Friday

The Chickasaw Nation has tribal jurisdiction over all of Stephens County, which may affect service of process in certain criminal and civil matters involving tribal members or land within the Chickasaw Nation reservation boundaries
Stephens County is part of Oklahoma's 5th Judicial District, which also includes Comanche, Cotton, and Jefferson counties
heroLead: Located in south-central Oklahoma, Stephens County is a vibrant community anchored by Duncan, the county seat, with deep roots in oil and gas production dating back to 1918. The county spans 891 square miles within the historic Chickasaw Nation and is home to approximately 44,000 residents who enjoy the Chisholm Trail Heritage Center, Lake Humphreys, and a diverse economy led by healthcare, education, and energy sectors. For reliable process serving throughout Stephens County, contact Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832 or visit our /contact page.

faqs.question: Where is the Stephens County Courthouse located and what are the hours?
faqs.answer: The Stephens County Courthouse is located at 101 S 11th Street, Duncan, Oklahoma 73533. The Court Clerk's office is in Suite 301 and can be reached at 580-470-2000. Office hours are Monday through Friday, 8:30am to 4:30pm. For process serving inquiries in Stephens County, call Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.

faqs.question: What is the history behind Stephens County's name?
faqs.answer: Stephens County was named for John Hall Stephens, a U.S. Congressman from Texas who strongly supported Oklahoma's push for statehood. The county was created at statehood in 1907, with Duncan designated as the county seat. The county was formed from approximately two-thirds of the former Pickens County, Chickasaw Nation, and one-third from Comanche County, Oklahoma Territory.

faqs.question: What major industries drive the Stephens County economy?
faqs.answer: Stephens County's economy is historically rooted in oil and gas production, with Halliburton Energy Services founded in Duncan in 1919. Today, the largest employment sectors include healthcare (Duncan Regional Hospital), education (Duncan and Marlow Public Schools), retail (Walmart, Family Dollar Distribution Center), and manufacturing. The county is known as the 'Buckle in the Oil Belt' due to its significant energy industry presence.

faqs.question: Does tribal jurisdiction affect legal matters in Stephens County?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Chickasaw Nation has full tribal jurisdiction over all of Stephens County as part of its 7,648-square-mile reservation. This may affect jurisdiction in certain criminal and civil matters involving Native American defendants or victims, as well as cases involving tribal land. The Chickasaw Nation operates the Chisholm Trail Casino in Duncan and maintains a Duncan Area Office at 1819 W. Plato Road. Process servers should be aware of potential jurisdictional considerations when serving tribal members.

faqs.question: What cities and towns are in Stephens County?
faqs.answer: Stephens County includes three cities: Duncan (the county seat), Marlow, and Comanche. Incorporated towns include Bray, Central High, Empire City, Velma, and Loco. Unincorporated communities include Alma, Corum, and Santa Fe. Duncan is the largest city with approximately 23,200 residents, followed by Marlow with around 4,400 residents.

faqs.question: What judicial district is Stephens County in and what does that mean?
faqs.answer: Stephens County is part of Oklahoma's 5th Judicial District, which also includes Comanche County, Cotton County, and Jefferson County. District court cases in Stephens County are heard at the Stephens County Courthouse in Duncan. The 5th Judicial District has jurisdiction over civil cases, felony criminal cases, probate matters, and juvenile cases. For assistance with legal document service in Stephens County, contact Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.
